titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Richard D. Weigle Papers
titlestmt.author: compiled by Robert Bartels

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday August 17, 2021 at 07:46

did.abstract: The collection consists of material related to Richard Weigle's time as a Yale-in-China bachelor and his service in the United States Army Air Force and the Chinese Army in India during World War II. The collection also contains material related to Richard Weigle's father, Luther Weigle.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of material related to Richard Weigle's time as a Yale-in-China bachelor and his service in the United States Army Air Force and the Chinese Army in India during World War II. The collection also contains material related to Richard Weigle's father, Luther Weigle. Included in the collection are family genealogies, photographs and negatives of the Weigle family and Richard's time in Asia and Europe, clippings pertaining to Richard and Luther Weigle, travel journals documenting Richard's time in Europe and China, a Yale-in-China diary kept by Richard Weigle, Weigle family correspondence, a World War II scrapbook, and Richard Weigle oral histories.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Richard D. Weigle was born in Northfield, Minnesota in 1912, and grew up in New Haven, Connecticut. After graduating from Yale University in 1931, he was a Yale-in-China bachelor from 1931 to 1933, teaching at Changsha in Hunan Province, China. He returned to China in 1935, and afterwards received a doctorate in diplomatic history from Yale University. He taught at Carleton College in Minnesota until leaving in 1942 to join the United States military. During World War II, Weigle served in the United States Army Air Force and the Chinese Army in India. After the war, Weigle was president of St. John's College in Annapolis, Maryland for thirty-one years. He died in Annapolis in 1992.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The papers are arranged in three groupings: Early life, China and later years, and Luther Weigle.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of the Mary Martha Weigle Estate, 2019.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Richard D. Weigle Papers (MS 2090). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research. Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. Researchers wishing to obtain an additional copy for their personal use should consult Copying Services information on the Manuscripts and Archives web site. Original computer files may not be accessed due to their fragility. Researchers must consult access copies.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright and other associated proprietary rights for unpublished materials authored or otherwise produced by Richard D. Weigle were transferred to Yale University in 2018. These materials may be used for non-commercial purposes without seeking permission from Yale University as the copyright holder. For other uses of these materials, please contact mssa.assist@yale.edu. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. Researchers wishing to obtain an additional copy of non-commercially produced items for their personal use should consult the ordering reproductions information on the Manuscripts and Archives web site.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. Researchers wishing to obtain an additional copy of non-commercially produced items for their personal use should consult the ordering reproductions information on the Manuscripts and Archives web site.

accessrestrict.p: Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. Researchers wishing to obtain an additional copy of non-commercially produced items for their personal use should consult the ordering reproductions information on the Manuscripts and Archives web site.

did.unittitle: Chinese Combat Command certificate of merit
Weigle family yearly Christmas letters and updates

odd.head: General
odd.p: Consists primarily of photocopies of original letters.

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: A related CD is housed separately for preservation purposes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Document containing description of contents in CD housed separtely.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: As a preservation measure, original materials may not be used. Digital access copies must be provided for use. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access.

did.unittitle: Intercollegiate Outing Club Association song fest
Oral history interview with Richard D. Weigle, transcript, and China articles

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Material provided from the donor indicated that this album is from Richard and Mary's first years, during the war. Mary stayed with her family, Luther, and Clara at Sunapee, Luther's summer home, until Richard returned after their daughter's birth.

did.unittitle: Weigle, Richard, World War II service
World War II service in Europe and Asia
China and World War II service
Slides, Weigle family trip to China
The Sugar Interests and American Diplomacy in Hawaii and Cuba, 1893-1903 , Yale dissertation by Richard D. Weigle
United States military patches and dog tag
United States military pins and belt buckle
Weigle, Luther Jr., and Jacqueline Sanborn Weigle
Yale-China association oral history of Richard D. Weigle
Bridal souvenir book for wedding of Luther Weigle and Clara Boxund
The Glory Days: From the Life of Luther Allan Weigle , by Richard D. Weigle
Knight of St. Gregory, papal award received by Luther Weigle
Photographs of Luther Weigle and family
Proceedings of the 99th annual convention of the Evangelical Lutheran Synod of West Pennsylvania
